core.label: Prolog Fundamentals
core.description: Facts, rules, queries, and logic programming.

topics.label: Installation (SWI-Prolog)
topics.description: Install SWI-Prolog and use the interactive top-level. Prolog is a logic programming language.

topics.label: Facts & Rules
topics.description: State facts (parent(tom, bob).) and define rules (grandparent(X,Z) :- parent(X,Y), parent(Y,Z).).

topics.label: Queries
topics.description: Ask questions with ?- queries. Prolog finds all solutions via backtracking.

topics.label: Unification
topics.description: Variables unify with values. Unification is Prolog's matching mechanism.

topics.label: Recursion
topics.description: Recursive rules with base cases. Prolog has no loops — recursion is the iteration mechanism.

topics.label: Lists
topics.description: [Head|Tail] notation, append, member, length, reverse. Lists are the primary data structure.

topics.label: Arithmetic
topics.description: is/2 for evaluation, comparison operators. Arithmetic is explicit with is.

topics.label: Cut & Negation
topics.description: ! (cut) to prune search, \+ for negation as failure. Use sparingly.
